title: Synthesize product/model/deployment/licensing decisions into roadmap
description: Integrate the current Loreweaver product, deployment, model-provider, billing, licensing, and UX decisions into architecture docs and existing beads epics/issues. Avoid parallel planning structures; create new issues only where no existing epic is a good home.
close_reason: Strategy synthesized into docs, ADR, parent acceptance criteria, existing epic notes, and scoped child issues.

title: E8 Dolt checkpoint/history/branching subsystem
description:
Goal: the Dolt-backed checkpoint / history / branching subsystem. SQLite stays the
authoritative live operational store; this subsystem makes Dolt the authoritative
checkpoint/history/branching store, OFF the per-turn hot path.

MVP must include (all in scope, none deferred):
- Deterministic SQLite -> Dolt checkpoint export (same SQLite state => identical bytes).
- A Dolt commit per meaningful checkpoint (session close; arc roll-up).
- List checkpoints.
- Restore a checkpoint into a NEW SQLite working copy.
- Fork a campaign from a checkpoint into a NEW Dolt branch (+ materialized SQLite copy).
- Explicit, verified separation from the beads Dolt storage / remotes / ref-namespace.

Hard constraints: Dolt is NEVER invoked in the per-turn write path; SQLite is the
single live truth (Dolt trails to last checkpoint, like a git working tree vs last
commit); there are NOT two independent live truths.

User-visible outcome: a player can checkpoint a campaign, list past checkpoints,
restore an old checkpoint into a fresh working copy, and fork a campaign timeline.
design:
## Architecture

Dolt is invoked via the `dolt` CLI ONLY at checkpoint time (no long-running
`dolt sql-server`, no per-turn calls) — this is what keeps it off the hot path and
avoids server-lifecycle complexity. Each campaign has its own Dolt repo directory,
created/located independently of the beads Dolt repo.

A `CheckpointStore` in core composes three lower units:
1. `serialize` — pure SQLite -> canonical snapshot (deterministic, no Dolt, offline-testable).
2. `doltRepo` — thin wrapper over the `dolt` CLI for one repo dir (the ONLY file that shells to `dolt`).
3. `separation` — guard proving the Loreweaver Dolt location/remote/ref-namespace is disjoint from beads.

## Determinism contract

`serialize(db)` reads every user table (excludes sqlite internal + any
`sqlite_%`), and emits a single canonical `snapshot.sql` string:
- tables ordered by name (ASCII);
- columns in `PRAGMA table_info` ordinal order;
- rows ordered by full-row tuple (stable total order);
- fixed value formatting (NULL as `NULL`, integers bare, text single-quoted with
  `''` escaping, blobs as `X'..'`).
Same SQLite content => byte-identical string (acceptance #8). This artifact is what
gets loaded into Dolt and committed, so `dolt_diff`/`AS OF` are meaningful.

## File map

- `packages/core/src/persistence/checkpoint/separation.ts` — `assertSeparateFromBeads(doltDir, beadsDir)`.
- `packages/core/src/persistence/checkpoint/serialize.ts` — `serializeCampaign(db): string`.
- `packages/core/src/persistence/checkpoint/doltRepo.ts` — `DoltRepo` (init, applySnapshot, commit, log, branch, checkoutInto).
- `packages/core/src/persistence/checkpoint/store.ts` — `CheckpointStore` (checkpoint, list, restoreToNewWorkingCopy, forkFromCheckpoint).
- `packages/core/src/index.ts` — barrel exports.
- `packages/core/test/checkpoint.*.test.ts` — one suite per unit.

## Hot-path / separation constraints

- `CheckpointStore` is invoked only by E6 session-close and E4 arc roll-up — never
  from `mutate_state` / the turn loop. This is a design rule enforced by where the
  call sites are (not by code in this epic).
- SQLite remains single live truth; restore/fork always materialize a NEW `.db`
  working copy and never mutate the live campaign DB in place.
- `separation` rejects any Dolt dir that is inside, equal to, or shares a remote
  URL / `refs/dolt/*` namespace with the beads Dolt repo.

## Test gating

`serialize` and `separation` are pure and run offline (no Dolt). `doltRepo` and
`CheckpointStore` suites use `describe.skipIf(!doltAvailable)` (probe `dolt version`)
so CI without the `dolt` binary stays green while the capability is proven where
`dolt` exists.

## Tradeoffs

The serializer is the deterministic export-mapping component Dolt-only would avoid;
accepted because it keeps the per-turn path embedded/zero-ops and yields a
diffable, byte-stable history at session granularity (the campaign's natural
versioning unit).
acceptance_criteria: 1. serializeCampaign is deterministic (same SQLite => byte-identical snapshot), proven offline. 2. A checkpoint creates a dolt commit; list returns it. 3. Restore materializes a NEW SQLite working copy round-trip-identical to the source. 4. Fork creates a NEW dolt branch + isolated SQLite copy; changes in fork do not affect origin. 5. Separation guard rejects any Dolt dir nested in/equal to/sharing remote or refs with the beads Dolt repo. 6. No checkpoint code path is reachable from the per-turn loop. All E8 child tasks closed.
notes: SYNTHESIS UPDATE 2026-05-17: Checkpoint/restore/fork remains a core text-first product differentiator and future demo surface. Keep Dolt checkpointing separate from both the per-turn path and beads storage; this supports restore/fork/history without early VTT/native-mobile scope.

title: Identify confirmed openly-licensed adventure for E2 conversion
description: RISK/BLOCKER for E2 conversion step (not schema work). D&D 5e SRD contains NO adventures. Must find a SPECIFIC adventure under CC-BY / ORC / OGL (community/indie, not a Wizards starter set). Output: named adventure + license + source. E2 schema/overlay/world_query can proceed in parallel; only the one-time conversion is gated on this.
acceptance_criteria: A specific adventure is named with source URL/location, license name/version, license text or authoritative reference, attribution requirements, commercial/hosted/redistribution allowances, and confirmation it is not fair-use-dependent or merely ownership-gated closed IP; E2 conversion can proceed only after these facts are recorded.
notes: SYNTHESIS UPDATE 2026-05-17: Legal source confirmation must reject fair-use-dependent or ownership-gated closed-IP adventures. Acceptable sources are explicitly open-licensed, public domain, original, or publisher-licensed, with attribution and allowed-use facts captured for pack metadata.

title: E3 Game-state subsystem + mutate_state tool
description: Canonical, exact, provenance-annotated game state - the trust anchor for the whole system. Owns the single canonical character schema (also the convergence target for future character import). mutate_state is the SOLE writer of canon and overlay facts.
design: Tables: character (single-row sheet), inventory (item rows), plot_flags, clock (in-game time + current location_id); every mutable row carries provenance + session_id + ts. mutate_state(target, field, op, value, provenance) validates, applies transactionally (turn atomic), records provenance; also the writer for overlay_facts (used by E2). No free model text ever writes canon. Crash-safety from per-turn transaction commit.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Canonical character/inventory/plot_flags/clock schema defined and documented as the single source. 2. mutate_state applies a change transactionally with provenance and rejects invalid ops. 3. Simulated crash mid-turn leaves last committed state intact and in-flight turn rolled back. 4. Provenance is queryable for narrative callbacks.
notes: SYNTHESIS UPDATE 2026-05-17: Canon-changing state mutations remain deterministic, transactional, provenance-backed, and tool-mediated. Cheaper/helper models may propose state deltas, but accepted canon changes require premium-model or deterministic validation before commit.

title: E1 SRD reference store + lookup_srd tool
description: Reusable, campaign-independent structured D&D 5e SRD data: monsters, spells, AND character-build options (races, classes/subclasses per SRD, backgrounds, feats, starting equipment, spell lists). Reframes the old heavy rules KB into structured lookup, not RAG. Consumed by E2 (srd_ref), E5 (running creatures), E7 (creation choices).
design: Read-only structured store (SQLite tables or bundled JSON loaded into a shared store). Stable ids referenced by srd_ref elsewhere. lookup_srd(ref-or-name, kind) tool returns exact stat blocks / build options. Built once from SRD 5.1 (CC-BY-4.0). NO fuzzy retrieval - exact keyed lookup only.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Monsters and spells queryable by name and ref with exact stat blocks. 2. Character-build options queryable. 3. lookup_srd returns structured results for a known monster, spell, and class build option. 4. Data sourced from CC-BY SRD 5.1 with license recorded.
notes: SYNTHESIS UPDATE 2026-05-17: SRD rules data remains a separate mechanics/reference store, not mixed into campaign lore or generated memory. License and attribution for SRD-derived data are required product behavior, not just documentation.

title: E0 Foundation: TS scaffold, core/CLI boundary, SQLite, Agent SDK
description: Foundational scaffold every other sub-epic builds on. TypeScript project, UI-agnostic core library vs thin CLI boundary, SQLite persistence layer (one DB file per campaign), Claude Agent SDK wiring, configuration.
design:
## Architecture

npm-workspaces monorepo. `@loreweaver/core` is the UI-agnostic library; `@loreweaver/cli`
is a thin consumer that depends on core (never the reverse). The workspace package
boundary is the enforced core/CLI seam (acceptance #1). TypeScript strict, ESM
(`"type": "module"`, `NodeNext`). Tests: vitest. SQLite: `better-sqlite3` (synchronous,
real transactions with automatic rollback on throw). Model access is isolated behind a
local `ModelClient` interface; an `AgentSdkModelClient` adapts the Claude Agent SDK so
the rest of the codebase never imports the SDK directly.

## Tech stack

- Node 24, TypeScript ^5.6, ESM/NodeNext, `tsc --build` project references.
- vitest ^2 for tests (`vitest run`).
- better-sqlite3 ^11 (+ @types/better-sqlite3).
- @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk (Claude Agent SDK, TypeScript) — installed via npm,
  wrapped behind `ModelClient`; exact SDK surface confined to one adapter file.

## File map

- `package.json` — root: workspaces, scripts (build/test/typecheck), shared devDeps.
- `tsconfig.base.json` — shared strict compiler options.
- `tsconfig.json` — root solution file (project references to both packages).
- `vitest.config.ts` — test discovery (`packages/**/test/**/*.test.ts`).
- `.env.example` — documents LOREWEAVER_DB_PATH, LOREWEAVER_MODEL, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.
- `packages/core/package.json` — core package + deps.
- `packages/core/tsconfig.json` — composite build config.
- `packages/core/src/index.ts` — public barrel: re-exports config, persistence, model, seams, `CORE_VERSION`.
- `packages/core/src/config.ts` — `LoreweaverConfig`, `ConfigError`, `loadConfig(env)`.
- `packages/core/src/persistence/db.ts` — `openDatabase(path)`, `withTransaction(db, fn)`.
- `packages/core/src/persistence/schema.ts` — `SCHEMA_VERSION`, `initSchema(db)` (meta table).
- `packages/core/src/model/client.ts` — `ModelClient`, `ModelMessage` interfaces.
- `packages/core/src/model/agentSdkClient.ts` — `AgentSdkModelClient` adapter (only file importing the SDK).
- `packages/core/src/seams.ts` — empty interface stubs: Orchestrator, ContextAssembler, ToolLayer, MemorySubsystem, WorldSubsystem, Persistence (filled by E1–E7).
- `packages/core/test/*.test.ts` — unit tests per module.
- `packages/cli/package.json` — cli package, depends on `@loreweaver/core`.
- `packages/cli/tsconfig.json` — composite, references core.
- `packages/cli/src/index.ts` — thin entry: `buildBanner`, `main` (prints version + resolved config).
- `packages/cli/test/cli.test.ts` — tests pure `buildBanner`.

## Boundaries / tradeoffs

- Core has zero dependency on cli (asserted by core building standalone).
- SDK API surface is uncertain at plan time; confined to `agentSdkClient.ts` and verified
  against installed types during T5. Unit tests use a fake `ModelClient`; the real
  round-trip is a single integration test gated on `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`.
- Seam interfaces are intentionally empty now; "typecheck" == `tsc --build` succeeds.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Core package builds with zero UI deps; CLI consumes core. 2. SQLite layer creates a campaign DB and passes a transactional write/rollback test. 3. Agent SDK wrapper makes a successful round-trip model call behind the interface. 4. Component interface stubs exist and typecheck.
notes: SYNTHESIS UPDATE 2026-05-17: E0 must keep Claude Agent SDK as an adapter only. The core model seam should grow toward provider adapters and model profiles (premium_dm, state_extractor, summarizer, rules_adjudicator, memory_reconciler, embedding_provider, economy_or_experimental). Initial config may still expose Anthropic/Claude settings, but naming and interfaces should avoid implying Claude is the only provider.

comments.author: Joseph Bongaarts
comments.text: Worktree baseline: fresh repo, no package.json/tests/build yet (scaffold is task cck.1). Branch work/loreweaver-cck clean at 1423dbe. No baseline checks applicable until cck.1 establishes the toolchain.

comments.author: Joseph Bongaarts
comments.text: SESSION HANDOFF (paused 2026-05-17): Subagent-driven execution of E0 in worktree D:\src\loreweaver\.worktrees\loreweaver-cck on branch work/loreweaver-cck. DONE+closed: cck.1 (scaffold), cck.2 (config), cck.3 (SQLite persistence) - all spec+quality reviewed. Branch HEAD 66362c4, worktree CLEAN, nothing in_progress. Ready next (per-task loop, one at a time, fresh subagent + spec-review then code-quality-review then bd close): cck.4 (seam stubs - brief already fetched, NOT yet claimed/started), then cck.5 (ModelClient+Agent SDK adapter), cck.6 (thin CLI; deps cck.1+cck.2 done). cck.7 = CI better-sqlite3-prebuild follow-up, infra, defer to finishing/CI phase (not in impl chain). After E0 fully closes, E8 loreweaver-7wc (Dolt checkpoint subsystem) unblocks: chain 7wc.1||7wc.2 -> 7wc.3 -> 7wc.4 -> 7wc.5 -> 7wc.6. Parent loreweaver-ve6 holds locked Hybrid-persistence design. NOT pushed/merged: feature branch is ephemeral (no upstream per bd context); E0 incomplete so do NOT merge to main yet. Resume by re-reading this, then continue subagent-driven-development per-task loop at cck.4.

title: Loreweaver: Agentic DM (solo persistent campaign MVP)
description:
Goal: a text-based agentic Dungeon Master that runs a single-player, persistent D&D campaign across many sessions, where the world changes over time and continuity is preserved within a bounded model-context budget.

User-visible outcome: from a local CLI, the player creates a character, starts/resumes/auto-closes play sessions, and converses with a DM agent that narrates, voices NPCs, adjudicates rulings, and advances an authored adventure while remembering the campaign across sessions.

Scope: MVP = solo persistent campaign on D&D 5e SRD content via the Claude Agent SDK (Pro plan), TypeScript. Non-goals (deferred): prose-rules RAG, semantic/RAG memory retrieval, general module importer, model-generated modules, web/multiplayer/long-running service, party/turn-taking, external character import.

This is an overarching epic decomposed into sub-epics E0-E7 (see Design).
design:
LOCKED DECISIONS:
- Rules: refined Hybrid. Model-trusted for narrative rulings; ALL math/dice/state via deterministic tools; structured SRD stat-block lookup (not fuzzy RAG); prose-rules RAG deferred.
- Memory: hierarchical pyramid (scene->session->arc->campaign_bible) as CORE; bounded always-on context independent of campaign length; anchored by exact provenance-annotated structured state re-asserted every turn; raw scene_log persisted from day one; semantic RAG deferred as future drill-down accelerator; never RAG-only.
- World: immutable authored module template (schema files) + per-campaign overlay-fact diff (latest-wins, git-like, provenance built in); module schema is the real deliverable; MVP seeds via ONE-TIME model-assisted conversion of ONE openly-licensed adventure; general importer deferred.
- Architecture: UI-agnostic TypeScript CORE library (Orchestrator, Context Assembler, Tool layer, Memory subsystem, World subsystem, Persistence) + THIN CLI front-end. Future web/multiplayer = new front-end on same core, deferred.
- Lifecycle: Campaign (long-lived) > Session (explicit start; AUTO-CLOSE on graceful exit runs recap + scene->session roll-up + state reconciliation + arc roll-up if milestone) > Scene (roll-up granularity, summaries written incrementally). Crash/hard-kill leaves session open; relaunch offers Resume vs Close&recap; canon crash-safe via transactional mutate_state + incremental scene summaries; close pipeline idempotent.
- Storage: HYBRID persistence (DECIDED, NOT deferred). SQLite is the AUTHORITATIVE LIVE operational store for active gameplay (embedded, per-turn ACID writes, one .db working copy per active campaign). Dolt is the AUTHORITATIVE checkpoint/history/branching store. At meaningful checkpoints (session close; arc roll-up) a DETERMINISTIC SQLite->Dolt export serializes all campaign tables and dolt-commits. Capabilities required in MVP: list checkpoints; restore a checkpoint into a NEW SQLite working copy; fork a campaign from a checkpoint into a NEW Dolt branch (+ materialized SQLite working copy). HARD CONSTRAINTS: Dolt is NEVER in the per-turn hot path; there are NOT two independent live truths (SQLite is the single live truth, Dolt trails to last checkpoint like a git working-tree vs last commit); Loreweaver Dolt storage/remotes/ref-namespace are EXPLICITLY separated from the beads Dolt repo (own dir, no piggybacking on refs/dolt/data). Authored module content remains version-controlled schema files imported at campaign creation.
- Language: TypeScript + official Claude Agent SDK (SDK is Python/TS only; TS chosen for schema typing, review legibility vs C#/Kotlin, shared types with future web front-end).

DECOMPOSITION (sub-epics, build order E0 -> E8 ; E1 || E3 ; -> E7 -> E4 -> E2 -> E5 -> E6):
- E0 Foundation: TS scaffold, core/CLI boundary, SQLite LIVE persistence, Agent SDK wiring, config. (Unchanged by Hybrid: SQLite remains the live store; persistence seam swappable.)
- E8 Dolt checkpoint/history/branching subsystem: deterministic SQLite->Dolt export, checkpoint commit, list checkpoints, restore-into-new-SQLite-working-copy, fork-from-checkpoint-into-new-branch, explicit beads-Dolt separation guard. NOT in per-turn path. (NEW, MVP, depends on E0.)
- E1 SRD reference store: structured monsters + spells + character-build options (races/classes/subclasses/backgrounds/feats/equipment/spell lists per SRD) + lookup_srd tool.
- E2 World subsystem: module schema, one-time model-assisted conversion of one open-licensed adventure, template + overlay-fact resolution, world_query tool. Conversion step gated on the legal-source blocker task; schema work not gated.
- E3 Game-state subsystem: canonical structured state tables (character/inventory/plot_flags/clock) with provenance, mutate_state tool as SOLE canon+overlay writer, transactional crash-safe writes.
- E4 Memory pyramid: scene_log (append-only), incremental scene_summary, session_recap + arc_summary roll-up steps, campaign_bible reconciliation, structural memory_drilldown tool.
- E5 Orchestrator + tools: turn loop, Context Assembler (bounded prompt = bible + arc + last recap + state snapshot + live scene transcript), roll, mark_scene, DM system prompt encoding the Hybrid rules contract.
- E6 Session lifecycle + CLI: campaign create/fork, start/resume/auto-close, idempotent close pipeline, thin CLI front-end. Session-close and arc roll-up INVOKE the E8 checkpoint export.
- E7 Character creation & import: MVP agent-guided conversational SRD-compliant creation with deterministic validation tools, output = canonical character record via mutate_state. Import (D&D Beyond/OCR/VTT) DEFERRED; all importers are mappers onto the single canonical character schema (E3-owned).

Data model summary in sub-epic E0/E2/E3/E4 designs; checkpoint serialization format in E8 design. Tradeoffs: hierarchy may smooth small details (mitigated by structured-state anchor + structural drill-down + persisted raw log); one-time conversion not reusable (accepted, importer deferred); Hybrid adds a deterministic export-mapping component (accepted in exchange for embedded zero-ops per-turn path + session-grain version graph; Dolt-only rejected because per-turn version resolution is undesirable so it would commit at session boundaries anyway, paying hot-path/server cost for no extra versioning benefit).
acceptance_criteria:
MVP is complete when, from the local CLI on D&D 5e SRD/open-licensed content:
1. Player can create an SRD-legal character via guided flow; sheet persists as canonical state.
2. Player can start a campaign forked from an authored module template; the converted open-licensed adventure loads.
3. Player can play multiple sessions; DM narrates, voices NPCs, adjudicates, runs creatures using looked-up stat blocks; all dice/math/state changes go through tools.
4. World changes (killed NPC, flipped plot flag, spent gold) persist as overlay/state and are reflected on later turns and sessions.
5. Graceful exit auto-closes the session (recap + roll-up); crash leaves session resumable with zero canon loss.
6. After many sessions, the DM demonstrates campaign continuity while assembled prompt size stays bounded and roughly constant with campaign length.
7. memory_drilldown retrieves a specific older session/scene on demand without it being permanently in context.
8. Session close and arc roll-up produce deterministic Dolt checkpoint commits; Dolt is never written in a per-turn path.
9. Player can list checkpoints, restore a checkpoint into a new SQLite working copy, and fork a campaign from a checkpoint into a new Dolt branch with an isolated working copy.
10. Loreweaver Dolt storage/remote/ref-namespace is verifiably disjoint from the beads Dolt repo.
11. Core model integration exposes provider-neutral adapters and model profiles; Claude/Anthropic remains an initial adapter, not a core assumption.
12. The primary DM path is configured for a premium_dm quality floor; economy models are not the default public demo or canonical DM unless explicitly marked experimental/validated.
13. Rules/mechanics, campaign content, live state, user-private packs, and generated memory remain separated with provenance.
14. Pack metadata can enforce license, attribution, hosted-use, sharing, redistribution, and monetization policy.
15. The demo path is premium-model, bounded-turn, and preloaded rather than economy-model-first.
16. Remaining deferred items are recorded explicitly: native VTT, native mobile, hosted billing/credits, general importers, and custom/local primary DM replacement.
All sub-epics E0-E8 closed; deferred items explicitly recorded, not silently dropped.
notes:
DB ARCHITECTURE DECIDED (user + second-model concurrence): HYBRID, not deferred. SQLite = authoritative live operational store (per-turn ACID, never replaced). Dolt = authoritative checkpoint/history/branching store. MVP must include: deterministic SQLite->Dolt checkpoint export; dolt commit per checkpoint; list checkpoints; restore checkpoint into NEW SQLite working copy; fork campaign from checkpoint into NEW Dolt branch; explicit separation from beads Dolt storage/remotes. Prohibitions: Dolt NOT in per-turn hot path; Dolt NOT deferred; NOT two independent live truths. New sub-epic E8 created for the Dolt checkpoint subsystem. E0 SQLite tasks UNCHANGED (SQLite stays the live store).
STRATEGY SYNTHESIS 2026-05-17: Product direction is text-first long-campaign AI DM for tabletop-seeking solo players and living text-world nostalgists. MVP/medium-term remains text-first; native VTT and native mobile are non-goals. CLI/local remains the near-term development and power-user surface; hosted mobile-friendly web/PWA is the likely medium-term public surface. Model strategy is provider adapters + model profiles, not Claude-only. premium_dm targets Opus 4.6+ / GPT-5.5-class or future equivalent; economy models are auxiliary/experimental unless validated. Billing starts BYOK-first; hosted credits are future optional. Public demo should be premium-model, short, bounded, and preloaded rather than economy-model-first. Content strategy separates rules, campaign content, live state, user-private content, and generated memory; public/bundled packs must be open, public-domain, original, or licensed; no fair-use-dependent public pack strategy or ownership-gated closed-IP derived packs. See docs/architecture-report.md and ADR 0001.

title: Add premium bounded demo campaign mode
description: Define a public demo path that uses premium_dm quality, a preloaded legal/original/open module state, and turn limits instead of lowering model quality. Demo should show memory, continuity, campaign state, and checkpoint behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Demo mode uses premium_dm by default, has a turn/session cap, loads only allowed-content packs, demonstrates continuity/state/checkpoints, and labels any economy-model run as experimental rather than intended quality.

title: Add provider adapter registry and model profile configuration
description: Extend the initial ModelClient seam into provider-neutral model profile configuration. Loreweaver should support profiles such as premium_dm, state_extractor, summarizer, rules_adjudicator, memory_reconciler, embedding_provider, and economy_or_experimental without assuming one provider handles every task. Claude Agent SDK remains one adapter, not a core assumption.
acceptance_criteria: Model profiles are represented in config/types; provider adapters can be selected per profile; premium_dm has explicit quality/capability expectations documented; no non-adapter core code imports Claude/Anthropic SDK APIs.

title: Build model evaluation harness and cost-quality metrics
description: Create an evaluation harness for model/profile quality gates. It should test long-campaign continuity, canon preservation, NPC consistency, rules adjudication, structured output reliability, memory update quality, tool-use reliability, cost per turn/session, and latency.
acceptance_criteria: Harness can run scripted scenarios against a ModelClient/profile; records continuity/canon/NPC/rules/structured-output/memory/tool-use scores plus cost and latency metrics; premium_dm pass/fail threshold is explicit and documented.

title: Add pack license and allowed-use metadata
description: Add license, attribution, provenance, and allowed-use metadata to the module/pack schema so product behavior can enforce whether content may be bundled, hosted, shared publicly, monetized, redistributed, or used only as user-private BYOK material.
acceptance_criteria: Pack schema includes licenseClass, licenseName, attribution, commercialUseAllowed, hostedUseAllowed, redistributionAllowed, publicSharingAllowed, derivativeAllowed, user-supplied/trademark flags, source description, provenance policy, and output restrictions; policy validation rejects unsupported closed/public-hosted uses.

title: Capture structured turn traces for future eval and helper models
description: Persist structured, consent-aware gameplay traces beyond raw transcripts so future evaluation, distillation, or helper-model training has accepted/rejected state deltas, provenance, corrections, and outcome labels.
acceptance_criteria: Trace records include campaignId, sessionId, turnId, playerInput, retrievedContext, prompt profile, model output, tool calls, rules resolution, accepted state delta, rejected candidates, final narration, memory updates, human corrections, and quality flags; storage keeps traces separate from public pack content.

title: CI: ensure better-sqlite3 installs without local C++ build tools
description: Risk surfaced in cck.3 code review: better-sqlite3 compiled from source on the dev machine (prebuild-install deprecated; no prebuilt matched Node 24 ABI). Any CI/clean environment without an MSVC/node-gyp toolchain will fail npm install. Resolve by pinning/using a better-sqlite3 version that ships NAPI prebuilds for the target Node ABI, or provisioning build tools in CI. Not blocking E0 dev; required before reproducible CI.
acceptance_criteria: CI installs and tests better-sqlite3 without requiring local C++ build tools on the supported runner; failure mode and fallback/prebuild strategy are documented; npm test/build remains green in CI.

title: Task 6: fork campaign from checkpoint into new branch
design:
Fork = `dolt branch <name> <checkpointId>` (a new named line rooted at the chosen
commit) + materialize a fresh SQLite working copy from that checkpoint via the
existing restore path. Isolation is structural: `readSnapshotAt` time-travels with
`AS OF '<commit-hash>'`, so the fork's working copy is bound to the exact commit,
not to any branch tip — later main-line checkpoints (cp2) cannot retroactively
change what the fork materializes. The test proves this by checkpointing a mutated
mainline AFTER forking and showing a re-materialized fork is byte-identical to the
original fork and free of the mainline change. `forkFromCheckpoint` reuses
`restoreToNewWorkingCopy` (DRY) so fork and restore share one materialize path.
acceptance_criteria: npx vitest run packages/core/test/checkpoint.fork.test.ts shows 1 passed with dolt or 1 skipped without; full checkpoint suite green/skip; npm run build exits 0.

title: Task 5: restore checkpoint into new SQLite working copy
design:
Restore reads the snapshot at a commit via Dolt `AS OF '<id>'` (time-travel),
parses the generic `campaign_snapshot` rows, and rebuilds a BRAND-NEW SQLite file
at `destDbPath` — the live campaign db is never touched (single live truth
preserved). Schema records are replayed first (original SQLite `CREATE` text, so
no MySQL/SQLite DDL mismatch), then rows are inserted from canonical JSON, with
`__blob` markers decoded back to Buffers. Round-trip fidelity is asserted by
comparing `canonicalize(serializeCampaign(...))` of source vs. restored — the same
deterministic function used for the history, so equality is exact. `id` is
single-quote-escaped into the `AS OF` literal (ids are dolt hashes/refs, but the
escape keeps it injection-safe by construction).
acceptance_criteria: npx vitest run packages/core/test/checkpoint.restore.test.ts shows 1 passed with dolt or 1 skipped without; prior checkpoint suites unchanged; npm run build exits 0.

title: Task 4: CheckpointStore checkpoint + list
design:
`CheckpointStore` is the public seam for E6/E4 call sites (session-close, arc
roll-up). Constructor enforces the beads separation guard up front so an
misconfigured campaign fails fast, before any Dolt repo is created. `checkpoint`
opens the live SQLite read-only-in-effect (only SELECTs via serialize), closes it
in `finally`, and never mutates it — SQLite stays the single live truth. It is a
plain synchronous call invoked OUTSIDE the turn loop. `list` is a thin pass-through
to `DoltRepo.log`. Restore/fork are added in E8.T5/T6 to keep each task one
coherent area.
acceptance_criteria: npx vitest run packages/core/test/checkpoint.store.test.ts shows 2 passed with dolt or 1 passed (separation) + 1 skipped without; npm run build exits 0.

title: Task 3: DoltRepo CLI wrapper
design:
Dolt is driven via `execFileSync('dolt', ...)` (argv form, no shell — injection-safe
for args; SQL values are single-quote-escaped via `sq`). No long-running
sql-server: every call is a short CLI invocation, so nothing here can sit in a
per-turn path. The generic `campaign_snapshot(tbl,kind,ordinal,payload)` table
sidesteps SQLite->MySQL DDL translation entirely while still giving Dolt real
table-level versioning (so `dolt_diff`/`AS OF` work in E8.T5). `--allow-empty`
keeps checkpointing idempotent when nothing changed. `available()` probes the
binary so suites can `skipIf` on machines/CI without dolt. `log --oneline` parse is
intentionally minimal (`<id> <message>`); if a future dolt changes oneline format,
only this method needs adjusting — the `Checkpoint` shape stays stable.
acceptance_criteria: npx vitest run packages/core/test/checkpoint.doltRepo.test.ts shows 2 passed with dolt installed or 1 passed + 1 skipped without; npm run build exits 0.

title: Task 2: deterministic SQLite snapshot serializer
design:
Determinism strategy avoids SQLite->MySQL DDL translation: rows are stored as
canonical JSON keyed by sorted column names; schema is captured verbatim as the
SQLite `CREATE` text (used on restore to rebuild the SQLite working copy, not fed
to Dolt). Row order is made physical-insert-independent by sorting the encoded JSON
strings; table order is `ORDER BY name`. Blobs are base64-wrapped so JSON is total.
`canonicalize` is a fixed field-order renderer so the determinism tests assert on
exact bytes. `SnapshotRecord[]` is the interchange type consumed by E8.T3
(`DoltRepo.applySnapshot`) and E8.T5 (restore). Pure + offline: runs in CI with no
dolt binary.
acceptance_criteria: npx vitest run packages/core/test/checkpoint.serialize.test.ts shows 3 passed (byte-identical regardless of insert order); npm run build exits 0. Pure/offline.

title: Task 1: beads-Dolt separation guard
design:
Pure path logic, zero Dolt/IO, so it runs in CI without the dolt binary. `isInside`
uses `path.relative`: empty (equal) or a non-`..`, non-absolute relative path means
containment. Checked both directions (dolt inside beads AND beads inside dolt) plus
equality. `BEADS_RESERVED_REF` is exported as the single source of the forbidden ref
namespace so DoltRepo (E8.T3) and any sync code refuse to reuse `refs/dolt/data`.
Remote-URL collision is enforced later in `DoltRepo` (it owns remote config); this
unit covers the filesystem and ref-namespace halves of the parent-epic separation
constraint.
acceptance_criteria: npx vitest run packages/core/test/checkpoint.separation.test.ts shows 5 passed; npm run build exits 0. Pure/offline (no dolt).

title: Task 6: Thin CLI consuming core (boundary proven)
design:
`buildBanner` is split out as a pure function so it is unit-testable without
spawning a process or capturing stdout. `main` is the only impure part and is
guarded by the `import.meta.url === file://argv[1]` check so importing the module
in tests does not execute it. The boundary proof (acceptance #1) is structural:
core has no dependency on `@loreweaver/cli` (verified by building core alone after
clearing dist), while cli imports `@loreweaver/core` — one-directional. CLI holds
zero game logic; it only formats and delegates to core's `loadConfig`. Banner text
`"Loreweaver — core v<version>"` must match the test exactly (em dash U+2014).
acceptance_criteria: npx vitest run packages/cli/test/cli.test.ts shows 1 passed; core builds alone after clearing dist; npm test passes all with model integration skipped.

title: Task 5: ModelClient interface + Agent SDK adapter
design:
Risk isolation: the exact Claude Agent SDK TypeScript surface is uncertain at plan
time, so the design forces ALL of it into `agentSdkClient.ts`. Everything else in
the codebase depends only on `ModelClient` (`complete(input) => Promise<string>`),
which is stable. Unit tests use an in-test `FakeModelClient` (no SDK, no network),
so the suite is deterministic and offline. Acceptance #3's "round-trip" is a single
integration test using `describe.skipIf(!ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)` so it runs only when a
key is present and never blocks CI/offline dev — but proves the real wrapper when
run with a key. The adapter's message-extraction logic is the expected adaptation
point: the implementer must check the installed package's exported types and adjust
only that block; the contract, barrel, and tests are unaffected by such changes.
`model` is injected via constructor for later swappability (parent-epic design:
"swappable model later").
acceptance_criteria: npx vitest run of model.test.ts and model.integration.test.ts shows 1 passed and 1 skipped when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is unset; npm run build exits 0.
notes: SYNTHESIS UPDATE 2026-05-17: Keep this task scoped to the initial Claude Agent SDK adapter, but avoid provider-specific leakage in the ModelClient contract. Follow-up provider/profile registry work is tracked separately so E0 can proceed without overloading this task.

title: Task 4: Component seam interface stubs
design:
Empty interfaces alone are not runtime-observable, so a `SEAMS` const array is the
runtime witness that makes acceptance #4 testable and lets later epics assert their
seam exists. Each sub-epic replaces the matching empty interface body with real
members (and keeps the name) so downstream imports never churn. `as const` + derived
`SeamName` union keeps the list and the type in lockstep. The genuine "typecheck"
gate is `npm run build` succeeding with these exported.
acceptance_criteria: npx vitest run packages/core/test/seams.test.ts shows 1 passed; npm run build exits 0 (seam stubs typecheck).

title: Task 3: SQLite persistence with atomic withTransaction
design:
`better-sqlite3` is synchronous, so the unit-of-work is a plain function — no
async/Promise. `db.transaction(fn)` returns a wrapped function that runs fn inside
`BEGIN`/`COMMIT` and automatically issues `ROLLBACK` if fn throws, then re-throws.
`withTransaction` simply invokes it, giving E3 (`mutate_state`) an atomic-per-turn
primitive. `:memory:` databases are used in tests (fast, isolated, supports
transactions). `Db` is re-exported as the canonical handle type so later epics
depend on this module, not on better-sqlite3 directly. `initSchema` is idempotent
(`IF NOT EXISTS` + `INSERT OR REPLACE`); E3/E4 will extend it with real tables.
WAL + foreign_keys pragmas set at open time for crash-safety/integrity per the
parent-epic design.
acceptance_criteria: npx vitest run packages/core/test/persistence.test.ts shows 3 passed including the rollback assertion; npm run build exits 0.
close_reason: SQLite persistence complete; spec-compliant and code-quality approved. Commits aa3e04e, 0d27b2b, 66362c4.

comments.author: Joseph Bongaarts
comments.text:
Code review summary for `6f2f3ff..66362c4`:

Assessment: Ready to merge

Critical:
- none

Important:
- none

Minor:
- `packages/core/src/persistence/schema.ts` — `initSchema` runs two separate implicit transactions (`db.exec(...)` then `db.prepare().run(...)`). If the process crashes between them the meta table exists but has no `schema_version` row. Because `initSchema` is idempotent the next call will repair it, so this is safe in practice; but wrapping both statements in a single `db.transaction(...)` call would make the intent explicit and guard against hypothetical future statements being inserted between them.
- `packages/core/src/persistence/db.ts:7` — WAL pragma silently degrades to `memory` journal mode on `:memory:` databases (confirmed: `pragma('journal_mode = WAL')` returns `[{journal_mode:'memory'}]`). The production code path (file DBs) is correct. The tests still validate all three real behaviors (commit, rollback, schema version). No test incorrectly asserts WAL mode is active, so this is a documentation/comment gap only, not a test accuracy issue.
- `packages/core/src/persistence/db.ts` — `openDatabase` lets `new Database(path)` throw natively on bad path (e.g., `TypeError: Cannot open database because the directory does not exist`). This is fine for a foundational primitive; just note that callers will need to handle or wrap this if user-facing error messages are required in E3/E8.
- `package-lock.json` — `prebuild-install` is marked deprecated in the lock file (`"deprecated": "No longer maintained…"`). `better-sqlite3` v11 still uses it as the prebuild download mechanism, falling back to `node-gyp rebuild` when no prebuilt matches. On this machine it compiled via node-gyp (no `prebuilds/` dir present). CI environments without C++ build tools will need either a `.npmrc` with `msvs_version` / a build container, or a later `better-sqlite3` version that ships napi prebuilds for Node 24. Not a blocker for merging (works locally, tests pass), but worth a follow-up chore before adding a CI pipeline.

Follow-up:
- none (prebuild/CI concern is a chore for the CI setup task, not a code change here)

comments.author: Joseph Bongaarts
comments.text: Code review 6f2f3ff..66362c4: Ready to merge. Critical: none. Important: none. Minor (deferred/optional): (1) initSchema uses two implicit txns - idempotent so safe; (2) WAL pragma no-ops on :memory: - doc-only; (3) openDatabase no error wrapping on bad path; (4) better-sqlite3 compiled from source (no prebuilt for Node24 ABI) -> CI without build tools will fail npm install (filed as follow-up). withTransaction correctly delegates to better-sqlite3 auto-rollback; Db abstraction leak-free; 3/3 tests real-behavior; build green. Both review gates passed.

title: Task 2: Core config loader with validation
design:
Signature: `loadConfig(env?: Record<string, string | undefined>): LoreweaverConfig`.
Defaulting `env` to `process.env` keeps callers simple while tests inject a fake env
(no global mutation). Empty/whitespace-only values are treated as missing via
`?.trim()` then falsy check. Model default is `claude-opus-4-7`; override via
`LOREWEAVER_MODEL`. `ConfigError` sets `.name` so callers/tests can branch on it.
This is the only config surface for E0; later epics extend `LoreweaverConfig` if needed.
acceptance_criteria: npx vitest run packages/core/test/config.test.ts shows 4 passed; npm run build exits 0.
close_reason: Config loader complete; spec-compliant and code-quality approved. Commit 6f2f3ff.

comments.author: Joseph Bongaarts
comments.text:
Code review summary for \`a997ea0..6f2f3ff\`:
Assessment: Ready to merge

Critical:
- none

Important:
- none

Minor:
- \`config.ts\` has no JSDoc on \`loadConfig\` or \`LoreweaverConfig\`; not a problem now, but this IS the only config surface for E0 and will be extended — one-line doc comments would pay forward.
- Test suite does not assert the error *message* string, only the class. Fine for now; if the message text is ever user-visible, add a \`.toThrow(/LOREWEAVER_DB_PATH is required/)\` assertion.
- \`DEFAULT_MODEL\` constant is module-private; if a future task needs the default (e.g., a CLI \`--help\` flag), it will re-invent it. Low risk at E0, worth noting.

Follow-up:
- none; whitespace-only env-var handling verified correct at runtime (trim + falsy collapse). \`instanceof ConfigError\` chain holds across module boundary. Build and 4/4 tests green.

comments.author: Joseph Bongaarts
comments.text: Code review a997ea0..6f2f3ff: Ready to merge. Critical: none. Important: none. Minor (deferred, optional): no JSDoc on loadConfig/LoreweaverConfig; tests assert ConfigError class not message; DEFAULT_MODEL module-private. Whitespace-only handling verified correct; ConfigError prototype chain holds; build + 4/4 tests green. Both review gates passed.

title: Task 1: Monorepo scaffold + vitest smoke test
description:
Objective:
Stand up the npm-workspaces monorepo (core + cli), TypeScript strict ESM build,
and vitest, proving the toolchain with one smoke test.

Files:
- Create: `package.json` (root)
- Create: `tsconfig.base.json`
- Create: `tsconfig.json` (root solution)
- Create: `vitest.config.ts`
- Create: `.env.example`
- Create: `packages/core/package.json`
- Create: `packages/core/tsconfig.json`
- Create: `packages/core/src/index.ts`
- Create: `packages/cli/package.json`
- Create: `packages/cli/tsconfig.json`
- Create: `packages/cli/src/index.ts`
- Test:   `packages/core/test/smoke.test.ts`

Steps:

1. Create `package.json` (root):
```json
{
  "name": "loreweaver",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "workspaces": ["packages/core", "packages/cli"],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "tsc --build",
    "typecheck": "tsc --build --force",
    "test": "vitest run"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/node": "^24.0.0",
    "typescript": "^5.6.0",
    "vitest": "^2.1.0"
  }
}
```

2. Create `tsconfig.base.json`:
```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "module": "NodeNext",
    "moduleResolution": "NodeNext",
    "strict": true,
    "declaration": true,
    "composite": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true
  }
}
```

3. Create `tsconfig.json` (root solution):
```json
{
  "files": [],
  "references": [
    { "path": "packages/core" },
    { "path": "packages/cli" }
  ]
}
```

4. Create `vitest.config.ts`:
```ts
import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config';

export default defineConfig({
  test: { include: ['packages/**/test/**/*.test.ts'] },
});
```

5. Create `.env.example`:
```
# Path to the SQLite database file for the active campaign
LOREWEAVER_DB_PATH=./campaigns/dev.db
# Claude model id used by the DM agent
LOREWEAVER_MODEL=claude-opus-4-7
# Anthropic API key for the Claude Agent SDK
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=
```

6. Create `packages/core/package.json`:
```json
{
  "name": "@loreweaver/core",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "type": "module",
  "main": "./dist/index.js",
  "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": { ".": "./dist/index.js" },
  "scripts": { "build": "tsc --build" }
}
```

7. Create `packages/core/tsconfig.json`:
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.base.json",
  "compilerOptions": { "rootDir": "src", "outDir": "dist" },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

8. Create `packages/core/src/index.ts`:
```ts
export const CORE_VERSION = '0.0.0';
```

9. Create `packages/cli/package.json`:
```json
{
  "name": "@loreweaver/cli",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "type": "module",
  "bin": { "loreweaver": "./dist/index.js" },
  "scripts": { "build": "tsc --build" },
  "dependencies": { "@loreweaver/core": "0.0.0" }
}
```

10. Create `packages/cli/tsconfig.json`:
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.base.json",
  "compilerOptions": { "rootDir": "src", "outDir": "dist" },
  "references": [{ "path": "../core" }],
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

11. Create `packages/cli/src/index.ts`:
```ts
import { CORE_VERSION } from '@loreweaver/core';

export function placeholder(): string {
  return `cli wired to core ${CORE_VERSION}`;
}
```

12. Write the failing smoke test `packages/core/test/smoke.test.ts`:
```ts
import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
import { CORE_VERSION } from '../src/index.js';

describe('toolchain smoke', () => {
  it('exposes a core version string', () => {
    expect(typeof CORE_VERSION).toBe('string');
    expect(CORE_VERSION.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
  });
});
```

13. Install dependencies.
   Command: `npm install`
   Expected: installs root devDeps and links workspaces, exit 0.

14. Run the smoke test (should now PASS — pure scaffold, no RED phase for pure config).
   Command: `npx vitest run packages/core/test/smoke.test.ts`
   Expected: 1 passed.

15. Verify the build (proves project refs + core/cli wire up).
   Command: `npm run build`
   Expected: exit 0, `packages/core/dist/index.js` and `packages/cli/dist/index.js` exist.

16. Commit.
   Command: `git add -A && git commit -m "feat(e0): npm-workspaces TS scaffold + vitest smoke test"`
design:
Scaffold-only task: no RED phase because there is no behavior yet — the "test"
is that the toolchain (install, vitest, tsc project-reference build) works end to end.

Key constraints:
- ESM everywhere: `"type": "module"`, `module`/`moduleResolution` = NodeNext.
  Relative imports in source/tests MUST use the `.js` extension (e.g.
  `../src/index.js`) even though the file is `.ts` — required by NodeNext.
- `composite: true` is mandatory for `tsc --build` project references.
- `@loreweaver/cli` depends on `@loreweaver/core` and never the reverse; npm
  workspaces symlink resolves the dependency.
- `.db` is already gitignored (campaign DBs are not committed).

This task only establishes structure; config/persistence/model/seam modules are
added by Tasks 2–6 which import from `packages/core/src`.
acceptance_criteria: npm install exits 0; npx vitest run packages/core/test/smoke.test.ts shows 1 passed; npm run build exits 0 with packages/core/dist/index.js and packages/cli/dist/index.js present.
close_reason: Scaffold complete; spec-compliant and code-quality approved. Commits caa915b, 2c3ba58, a997ea0.

comments.author: Joseph Bongaarts
comments.text:
Code review summary for `1423dbe..2c3ba58`:
Assessment: Ready to merge

Critical:
- none

Important:
- `packages/core/package.json` exports map uses only the bare string `"./dist/index.js"` with no condition keys (`import`/`default`). NodeNext consumers can resolve this, but when later tasks add CJS interop or type-only re-exports the lack of a `types` condition in the exports map will require a breaking change. Recommendation: expand now to `{ ".": { "import": "./dist/index.js", "types": "./dist/index.d.ts" } }` so the `types` field inside `exports` is authoritative for TypeScript ≥5.0 `bundler`/NodeNext resolution (the top-level `"types"` key is a fallback).
- `.env` is not gitignored. `.env.example` is committed (correct), but if a developer creates `.env` from it the actual secrets file will be untracked but not ignored — no protection against accidental `git add`. Add `.env` to `.gitignore` in the same chore commit or a follow-up.

Minor:
- `package.json` has no `engines` field. The repo is running Node 24; later tasks will use Node-24-only APIs (e.g. the Anthropic Agent SDK). Adding `"engines": { "node": ">=22" }` now documents the minimum and npm will warn on mismatched installs.
- `package.json` has no `packageManager` field. Adding `"packageManager": "npm@11.12.1"` (current version) enables corepack enforcement and signals intent to future contributors.
- `vitest.config.ts` glob `packages/**/test/**/*.test.ts` will silently skip a test file placed in e.g. `packages/core/src/__tests__/foo.test.ts`. The pattern is fine for the current layout; just document the convention (tests go in `test/`) somewhere visible (CLAUDE.md architecture section) before task 2 adds more tests.
- `typecheck` script uses `tsc --build --force` which re-compiles all packages regardless of cache, making it slow at scale. It is a reasonable escape hatch but the name `typecheck` implies type-checking-only (no emit), whereas `--force` still emits. Consider `tsc --build --noEmit` or renaming to `rebuild`.

Follow-up:
- loreweaver-cck.2+ authors should be aware: adding new source files to `packages/core/src` requires no tsconfig change (glob via `include: ["src"]`), but test files outside `packages/*/test/` will not be picked up by vitest — document in CLAUDE.md.
- Expand `exports` map in core before first public-facing release (task 6 or sooner).
- Add `.env` to `.gitignore` (can be done in task 2 chore).

comments.author: Joseph Bongaarts
comments.text: Re-review 1423dbe..a997ea0: Ready to merge. Critical: none. Important: none (both prior Important issues - conditional exports map + .env gitignore - resolved in a997ea0, no regressions, no scope creep). Minor: deferred (engines/packageManager/typecheck) intentionally not addressed. Both review gates passed.

title: E6 Session lifecycle + thin CLI front-end
description: Player-facing wrapper: campaign create/fork, Campaign/Session/Scene lifecycle with explicit start and AUTO-CLOSE on graceful exit, idempotent close pipeline, crash-safe resume, thin CLI over the core.
design: Session states open/closed; at most one open session per campaign. Launch: if open session exists offer Resume (reattach, reload scene tail) or Close-and-recap; else Start new. Graceful exit runs close pipeline: final scene_summary, session_recap, state reconciliation, arc roll-up if milestone, mark closed. Crash: session stays open, only current unsummarized scene tail volatile (survives in scene_log), no canon loss. Close pipeline idempotent. CLI is a thin presentation layer (campaign select/create, prompt I/O, streaming, resume prompt) with no game logic.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Create/fork a campaign from a module template via CLI. 2. Start, play, graceful-exit auto-runs full close pipeline. 3. Simulated crash leaves session open; relaunch offers Resume and Close-and-recap; Resume reloads scene tail with zero canon loss. 4. Re-running close pipeline after partial failure produces no duplication/corruption. 5. CLI contains no game-rule logic.
notes: SYNTHESIS UPDATE 2026-05-17: E6 remains CLI/local-first. CLI is the near-term power-user/dev surface and should keep game logic in core. Medium-term public UX is a hosted mobile-friendly web/PWA over the same core; native mobile is deferred. BYOK is the default early billing shape. Hosted BYOK requires separate secret-handling design; hosted credits are future optional and not an MVP blocker.

title: E5 Orchestrator + turn loop + Context Assembler + tools
description: The integrating loop: per-turn cycle via Agent SDK, bounded-context assembly, deterministic tools (roll, mark_scene), and the DM system prompt encoding the refined-Hybrid rules contract.
design: Orchestrator: player text to Context Assembler builds bounded prompt (campaign_bible + current arc_summary + last session_recap + full structured-state snapshot + current scene live transcript) to Agent SDK call to execute tool calls deterministically to stream narration to append scene_log to (if scene boundary) write scene_summary. Tools: roll(dice,reason) code-owned RNG, mark_scene(boundary,title). Integrates lookup_srd (E1), mutate_state/overlay (E3/E2), memory_drilldown (E4). System prompt: DM persona + Hybrid contract (narrate freely; all math/dice/state via tools; lookup_srd before running any creature). Turn atomic; tool error returns structured error to model; model/SDK failure leaves pre-turn state intact.
acceptance_criteria: 1. A full player turn runs end to end: prompt assembled, model called, tools executed, narration streamed, scene_log appended. 2. Assembled prompt contains only the bounded set, excluding older history unless drilled down. 3. roll RNG is code-owned and reproducible under seed in test. 4. Model attempting to change state via prose (not tool) does not mutate canon. 5. lookup_srd invoked before a creature is run in a combat scenario test.
notes: SYNTHESIS UPDATE 2026-05-17: E5 owns bounded context and model quality discipline. The Context Assembler must avoid whole-history prompts, use compact active-scene/state/memory slices, and leave room for provider prompt caching where available. Turn orchestration should emit cost/latency/quality instrumentation by model profile and support an evaluation harness for premium_dm continuity, canon, NPC consistency, structured output, rules, tool use, and memory-update quality.

title: E2 World subsystem: module schema, conversion, overlay, world_query
description: Module schema (the real deliverable), one-time model-assisted conversion of ONE openly-licensed adventure, immutable template plus per-campaign overlay-fact resolution, world_query tool. General importer deferred.
design: Module schema files: meta, locations[], encounters[] (creatures via srd_ref to E1), npcs[], triggers[] (plot advancement), lore[]. Imported into campaign DB at creation; authored files stay pristine for re-forking. overlay_facts (written by E3 mutate_state): target_type/target_id/field/value/provenance/session/ts; current world = template plus latest-wins overlay. world_query(target) resolves template+overlay so the model never narrates a stale template. Conversion step is a one-time model-assisted content task, NOT a reusable parser; it is gated on the confirmed-legal-source blocker task. Schema + overlay + world_query work is NOT gated and proceeds first.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Module schema defined and documented. 2. One openly-licensed adventure converted into schema-conforming files (after legal source confirmed). 3. Campaign creation forks template into a campaign DB leaving authored files unchanged. 4. world_query returns template+overlay-resolved truth; an overlay fact (e.g., NPC killed) overrides the template on later queries.
notes: SYNTHESIS UPDATE 2026-05-17: E2 also owns pack license/allowed-use metadata and user-private pack posture. Shipped/public packs must be open, public-domain, original, or publisher-licensed; unsupported closed-IP derived packs must not be shipped or merely ownership-gated. Module schema should preserve rules/campaign/live-state/user-private/generated-memory separation and expose metadata that can enforce hosting/sharing/monetization policy.

title: E4 Memory pyramid + roll-up + structural drill-down
description: Hierarchical memory as the CORE continuity mechanism: scene to session to arc to campaign_bible, bounded always-on context independent of campaign length, anchored by E3 structured state. Raw scene_log persisted for future RAG (deferred).
design: Tables: scene_log (append-only raw), scene_summary (incremental, salient_refs linking to state/overlay rows), session_recap, arc_summary, campaign_bible (standing world_facts, major_npcs, factions, open_threads). Roll-up steps are separate focused agent invocations writing strictly upward, each citing source ids (re-runnable, idempotent). memory_drilldown(selector) pulls a specific older summary/log slice into context on demand only. Semantic/RAG retrieval DEFERRED - structural drill-down only for MVP.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Scene summaries written incrementally during play. 2. Session close produces a recap from scene summaries plus state delta. 3. Arc milestone folds recaps into arc_summary and reconciles campaign_bible. 4. Roll-up idempotent (re-run yields no corruption). 5. memory_drilldown returns a specific past scene/session without it being permanently in context. 6. Always-on memory footprint stays bounded as session count grows.
notes: SYNTHESIS UPDATE 2026-05-17: Memory rollups should also preserve structured turn traces useful for future eval/helper-model training: retrieved context, prompt profile, model output, tool calls, accepted/rejected state deltas, memory updates, corrections, and quality flags. Raw transcripts alone are not the valuable future dataset.

title: E7 Character creation and import
description: MVP: agent-guided conversational, SRD-legal character creation producing the canonical character record. Deferred: import from D&D Beyond, OCR-scanned paper sheets, VTT exports - all map onto the single canonical schema (E3-owned), never forking the model.
design: Creation: conversational flow where the model proposes choices and deterministic validation tools enforce legality (class/race/stat/equipment combos, point-buy/standard-array math, spell selection). Output written via mutate_state as canonical state. Reuses E1 build options. Import: explicitly deferred; mandate is one canonical character schema with future importers as pure mappers onto it (documented, not built).
acceptance_criteria: 1. A new player completes guided creation ending with an SRD-legal sheet persisted as canonical state. 2. Validation tool rejects illegal combinations and bad point-buy/array math. 3. Created character is compatible with the E3 canonical schema. 4. Import documented as deferred with the mapper-onto-canonical-schema mandate recorded.

title: Brainstorm: Loreweaver agentic Dungeon Master
description: Explore requirements and produce an approved feature epic for an agentic DM: reusable rules KB, campaign KB (template + instances), cross-session memory, DM agent on Claude Agent SDK, deterministic tools.
notes:
MVP target: Solo persistent campaign - single player, small campaign across many sessions, persistent memory, world mutates over time. Exercises memory + campaign-state subsystems from day one.
Rules approach DECIDED: refined Hybrid. (1) Model-trusted for narrative rule adjudication. (2) All math/dice/state changes via deterministic tools. (3) Structured SRD monster/spell stat-block lookup tool (queryable data store, NOT fuzzy RAG). (4) Prose-rules RAG DEFERRED until proven necessary in real play (YAGNI for MVP). Reframes 'reusable rules KB' as lightweight structured stat-block store + lookup tool.
Memory model DECIDED: Hierarchical memory as CORE architecture. Pyramid: scene->session->arc->campaign-bible summaries, rolled up by reasoning steps. DM always holds bounded top-of-pyramid in context; drill-down on demand via tool. Anchored by EXACT annotated/provenance-bearing structured state (re-asserted every turn, anti-drift). Raw scene log persisted from day one. Semantic retrieval (RAG) DEFERRED - added later only as drill-down accelerator if real play shows pinpoint-recall gaps. Never RAG-only. Context budget bounded regardless of campaign length - key requirement (user: continuity over long campaigns is top priority).
Campaign content DECIDED: Real deliverable = structured module SCHEMA (locations[], keyed encounters[], npcs[], triggers/plot_flags[], lore[]). MVP seeds it via ONE-TIME model-assisted conversion of ONE openly-licensed adventure - content task, NOT a reusable parser. General importer DEFERRED. World mutation = immutable authored module (template) + per-campaign diff/overlay = current world (git-like, parallel to structured-state design). OPEN QUESTION/RISK: must identify a specific confirmed openly-licensed (CC-BY/ORC/OGL) adventure - SRD itself has NO adventures. Lost Mine of Phandelver etc. are NOT open-licensed.
Interface DECIDED: Local CLI as THIN presentation layer over a UI-agnostic CORE library (game loop, agent orchestration, memory, storage, tools). Future web/multiplayer = new front-end on same core (DEFERRED but unblocked by this boundary). Model: Campaign (long-lived) > Session (explicit open; AUTO-CLOSE on graceful exit runs recap+scene->session roll-up+state reconciliation) > Scene (roll-up granularity, summaries written incrementally during play). Crash/non-graceful exit leaves session OPEN; relaunch offers Resume vs Close&recap. Canon crash-safe via live tool state writes + incremental scene summaries; only current scene transcript tail volatile.
Language DECIDED: TypeScript with official Claude Agent SDK. Rationale: Agent SDK is Python/TS-only (no official C#/Kotlin); user dislikes Python, native C#/Kotlin, reviews-not-writes code; TS static typing strong for schema-heavy core (module schema, annotated structured state, pyramid nodes, overlay diff); reads close to C#/Kotlin for review; shares types with future web front-end. Polyglot/raw-API-in-native REJECTED as premature/off-norm.
DESIGN APPROVED by user (with tweaks incorporated). Tweak: added E7 Character creation & import; broadened E1 to SRD reference store (creatures+spells+character-build options). Creation = agent-guided conversational + deterministic validation tools, MVP. Import (D&D Beyond, OCR paper, VTT) DEFERRED, all map onto one canonical character schema (E3-owned). Proceeding to create overarching epic + E0-E7 sub-epics.
Epics created: parent loreweaver-ve6; sub-epics E0=cck E1=zzy E3=c07 E7=ay3 E4=wsv E2=hlu E5=ws9 E6=ua0; legal-source risk task loreweaver-40w. Dependencies wired for build order E0 -> (E1||E3) -> E7 -> E4 -> E2 -> E5 -> E6; parent depends on all sub-epics.
close_reason: Brainstorm complete; design approved; overarching epic loreweaver-ve6 + sub-epics E0-E7 + risk task created and approved by user. Transitioning to implementation planning for E0 (loreweaver-cck), the only unblocked epic.

title: Design hosted web/PWA BYOK deployment path
description: Capture the medium-term public deployment shape: hosted mobile-friendly web/PWA over the same core, with local BYOK preserved and hosted BYOK secret handling designed before launch. Native mobile and hosted credits are deferred.
acceptance_criteria: Design describes CLI/local, hosted web/PWA, local BYOK, hosted BYOK secret handling, and future hosted credits; explicitly defers native mobile; stored provider keys are never exposed back to the browser after storage.
